Container.Name: uup-dump-windows-iso
Container.Overview: Downloads and creates the latest Windows ISO using UUP dump. Checks if an ISO with the same build number already exists before downloading — if it does, it logs and exits immediately. Supports Windows 10, Windows 11, and Windows Server 2022.
Container.Description: Downloads and creates Windows ISO files from UUP dump.[br][br] On each run the container:[br] 1. Fetches the latest build number from UUP dump[br] 2. Checks if an ISO for that build already exists in the output folder[br] 3. If yes — logs and exits immediately (no unnecessary download)[br] 4. If no — downloads all Windows Update packages and converts them to an ISO[br][br] ISOs are named: {major}.{minor}.Vibranium-X64-{LANG}-{EDITION}_Updated.iso[br][br] Temporary files (~30 GB during build) are stored inside the container under /output/.work and cleaned up automatically when the ISO is done or if an error occurs.[br][br] Schedule via the User Scripts plugin to check for new builds monthly.

Config.Name: ISO Output Path
Config.Description: Directory where the finished ISO files and log will be saved. Temporary build files (~30 GB) are also stored here under .work/ and cleaned up automatically.

Config.Name: Windows Target
Config.Description: Which Windows version to build: windows-11, windows-10, or windows-2022

Config.Name: Language
Config.Description: Language pack, e.g. de-de, en-us, fr-fr, es-es, it-it, pl-pl

Config.Name: Edition
Config.Description: Windows edition: Professional, Home, ServerStandard, ServerDatacenter

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID for file ownership (Unraid nobody = 99)

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for file ownership (Unraid users = 100)
